rulings.Case Title: FOROUGH NADAF-RAHROV VS. NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Quash Pltf'S "Amended Ntc To Deft Neiman Marcus Group, Inc. To Attend Trial" And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 5, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC.'S MOTION To Quash Plaintiff's "Amended Ntc To Deft Neiman Marcus Group, Inc. To Attend Trial" And For Sanctions. GRANTED AS TO NINA KERN AND ALL OTHER BOARD MEMBERS. WHETHER MS. KERN IS TO BE AVAILABLE FOR PLAINTIFF'S CASE-IN-CHIEF IF DEFENDANT CALLS HER FOR ITS CASE-IN-CHIEF IS AN ISSUE TO BE ADDRESSED BY THE TRIAL COURT. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE NOTICE WAS SENT IN BAD FAITH, ONLY TO HARASS, OR THAT PLAINTIFF OPPOSED THIS MOTION WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC et al (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 5, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS MUST CLARIFY WHAT CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ASSERTED AGAINST FEDEX ON A RESPONDEAT SUPERIOR THEORY SUCH THAT IT IS CLEAR AGAINST WHICH DEFENDANTS PLAINTIFF ASSERTS A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC et al (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notcie Of Motion And Moition To Stay All Civil Proceedings Or, In The Alternative, To Stay All Discovery Pending Resolution Of Parallel Criminal Proceedings; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 5, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Stay All Civil Proceedings Or, In The Alternative, To Stay All Discovery Pending Resolution Of Parallel Criminal Proceedings. MOTION TO STAY ALL CIVIL PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED. MOTION TO STAY ALL DISCOVERY PENDING RESOLUTION OF PARALLEL CRIMINAL PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC et al (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 5, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO ASSERT A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST FEDEX, THE HEADING FOR EACH CAUSE OF ACTION SHOULD UNAMBIGUOUSLY SO STATE. (CRC 2.112.) DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. IF THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALLEGED AGAINST FEDEX, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT SHOW A DUTY TO SUPERVISE/OVERSEE WAS OWED, THE DUTY WAS BREACHED, HOW IT WAS BREACHED, AND THE BREACH WAS THE FACTUAL AND PROXIMATE CAUSE OF DAMAGES TO PLAINTIFF. THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION MUST SPECIFY WHICH ACTS OF LANGUBAN IT IS ASSERTING FEDEX HAS LIABILITY FOR. =(302/CWW)
